Package space.sunqian.fs.utils.jdbc

Interface SqlBuilder

public interface SqlBuilder
This interface is used for building SQL in method chaining. For example: 

 // simple sql:
 List<User> users = SqlBuilder.newBuilder()
     .append("SELECT * FROM users WHERE 1=1)
     .append(" AND gender = ", gender)
     .append(" AND id in (", idList).append(")")
     .build()
     .connection(connection)
     .query(User.class);
 // batch sql:
 BatchSql batchSql = SqlBuilder.newBuilder()
     .append("INSERT INTO users (name, age) VALUES (?, ?);")
     .buildBatch()
     .batchParameters(batchParams)
     .connection(connection)
     .execute();
See Also:
  • PreparedSql

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    @Nonnull SqlBuilder
    append(@Nonnull String sql)
    Appends a raw string to the current SQL.

    Appends a parameterized string to the current SQL.

  • Method Details

    • newBuilder

      @Nonnull static @Nonnull SqlBuilder newBuilder()
      Returns a new instance of SqlBuilder.
      Returns:
      a new instance of SqlBuilder

    • append

      @Nonnull @Nonnull SqlBuilder append(@Nonnull @Nonnull String sql)
      Appends a raw string to the current SQL.
      Parameters:
      sql - the given raw string to append
      Returns:
      this builder

    • append

      @Nonnull @Nonnull SqlBuilder append(@Nonnull @Nonnull String sql, @Nullable @Nullable Object param)
      Appends a parameterized string to the current SQL.

      This method first appends the given raw string, and then appends the question mark (?) for parameter binding. If the given parameter is not an instance of Iterable, this method appends one question mark (?), otherwise, this method appends multiple question marks joined by commas (?,?,?...), and the number of question marks is equal to the size of the Iterable parameter.

      Parameters:
      sql - the given raw string to append
      param - the given parameter value to bind
      Returns:
      this builder

    • appendIf

      @Nonnull default @Nonnull SqlBuilder appendIf(boolean condition, @Nonnull @Nonnull String sql)
      Conditionally appends a raw string to the current SQL.

      The given raw string will only be added if the specified condition evaluates to true.

      Parameters:
      condition - the condition that determines whether to append the SQL
      sql - the given raw string to append if condition is true
      Returns:
      this builder

    • appendIf

      @Nonnull default @Nonnull SqlBuilder appendIf(boolean condition, @Nonnull @Nonnull String sql, @Nullable @Nullable Object param)
      Conditionally appends a parameterized string to the current SQL.

      The given parameterized string will only be added if the specified condition evaluates to true. If it is, this method first appends the given raw string, and then appends the question mark (?) for parameter binding. If the given parameter is not an instance of Iterable, this method appends one question mark (?), otherwise, this method appends multiple question marks joined by commas (?,?,?...), and the number of question marks is equal to the size of the Iterable parameter.

      Parameters:
      condition - the condition that determines whether to append the SQL
      sql - the given raw string to append if condition is true
      param - the given parameter value to bind if condition is true
      Returns:
      this builder

    • build

      @Nonnull @Nonnull PreparedSql build()
      Builds and returns the final prepared SQL.
      Returns:
      the prepared SQL ready for execution

    • buildBatch

      @Nonnull @Nonnull PreparedBatchSql buildBatch()
      Builds and returns the final prepared SQL for batch execution. The returned PreparedBatchSql object is only built from the current prepared SQL string, and the previously added parameters will be discarded. The actual batch parameters should be added by PreparedBatchSql.batchParameters(List) or PreparedBatchSql.parameters(List).
      Returns:
      the prepared SQL ready for execution for batch execution
